problem.title: Find Leaves of Binary Tree
problem.description: Given the root of a binary tree, collect a tree's nodes as if you were doing this: Collect all the leaf nodes.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – DFS with Height
solutions.description: Assign each node a 'height' defined as the length of the longest path from that node to a leaf (leaves have height 0). Nodes at the same height belong to the same collection group. The DFS computes this height bottom-up and places each node's value into the corresponding index of the result list, naturally grouping leaves first.
solutions.code_python:
# class TreeNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, left=None, right=None):
#         self.val = val; self.left = left; self.right = right
from typing import List, Optional

def findLeaves(root) -> List[List[int]]:
    result: List[List[int]] = []

    def get_height(node) -> int:
        if node is None:
            return -1
        left_h = get_height(node.left)
        right_h = get_height(node.right)
        current_h = max(left_h, right_h) + 1
        if current_h >= len(result):
            result.append([])
        result[current_h].append(node.val)
        return current_h

    get_height(root)
    return result

# class TreeNode:

from typing import List, Optional
